body: Cycle 1 review-spec verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES (1 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM, 1 LOW). Cycle 2 amendments: ac-01 LOW addressed (already named the table as trimmable; reviewer missed it but tightened wording); ac-02 amended to declare 'How to update' line participates in byte-for-byte comparison; ac-03 amended to (a) pin byte-for-byte comparison rule and (d) require legacy-CLAUDE.md detection + migration prompt for the existing ## Workflow (orbit) / ## Orbit vocabulary / ## Current Sprint blocks; ac-04 amended to add a second new scenario for legacy-migration; ac-05 amended to specify @.orbit/STYLE.md import stays alongside @.orbit/METHOD.md (STYLE.md not absorbed); ac-06 amended to name 'orbit spec note' as the audit-record mechanism; ac-07 amended to add a legacy-project test scenario (e/f/g); ac-08 amended to name 'orbit spec note' as the principle-record mechanism; new ac-09 declares implementation order so /orb:implement claims ACs in a non-dangling sequence (ac-01 → ac-02 → ac-03 → ac-05 → ac-06 → ac-04 → ac-07 → ac-08).

Cycle 2 review-spec verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES (1 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM, 1 LOW). Cycle 3 amendments: ac-01 — BLUF skeleton inlined directly (TL;DR/Recommendation/Why/Detail/Confidence) instead of pointing at .orbit/STYLE.md; eliminates the dangling-pointer problem for downstream projects without STYLE.md. ac-03 — restructured to atomic semantics: legacy-detection happens BEFORE @-import addition; decline path REFUSES to add @-import (no dual-source drift); marker heading dropped (matches existing @.orbit/STYLE.md shape). ac-04 — added explicit pillar 2 attribution via relations:feeds → 0028. ac-05 — STYLE.md handling clarified: orbit-repo's @.orbit/STYLE.md import stays as the richer expanded form; downstream projects get the inlined BLUF skeleton via METHOD.md (per ac-01), not a STYLE.md template. ac-07 — split into three explicit-interaction-mode scenarios (non-interactive fresh, assertion-only drift prompt, scripted-answer legacy migration with both accept and refuse paths). ac-09 — implementation order swapped: ac-06 now precedes ac-05 (audit determines what CLAUDE.md content survives the dogfood). Goal updated to add pillar attribution sentence.

Cycle 3 deviation applied inline: ac-03 reordered to detect-before-copy (no orphan METHOD.md on refuse); ac-04 names relations:feeds → 0028 as canonical pillar attribution (no separate pillars: shorthand); ac-05 / ac-06 ownership seam pinned (ac-06 produces audit verdict spec-note only, ac-05 lands the edits); ac-07 sub-asserts relabelled t1/t2/t3 to avoid collision with ac-03's a/b/c/d; ac-09 updated to reflect the seam.

ac-06 audit verdict for orbit-repo CLAUDE.md 'Session Completion / Mandatory Workflow' section (lines 93-117). Rule-by-rule classification:

(i) substrate-shaped — DELETE from CLAUDE.md, covered by METHOD.md substrate rules:
- 'File tasks for remaining work' → orbit task open
- 'Update task status' → orbit task done / update
- 'Hand off' → orbit memory remember <session-handoff-key>

(ii) project-specific git/CI discipline — KEEP in orbit-repo CLAUDE.md as a tight block:
- 'Run quality gates (if code changed)' — tests/linters/builds (project-specific stack)
- 'PUSH TO REMOTE' — git pull --rebase && git push, status must show 'up to date with origin'
- 'Clean up' — stashes, remote branch pruning
- 'Work is NOT complete until git push succeeds' — load-bearing repo policy
- 'If push fails, resolve and retry until it succeeds'

(iii) stale (restating other rules) — DELETE entirely:
- 'Verify — all changes committed AND pushed' (folded into push rule)
- 'NEVER stop before pushing' (restating)
- 'NEVER say ready to push when you are' (restating)

ac-05 will land these edits: replace the entire 93-117 block with a tight ~3-5 line 'Push discipline' section covering only category (ii).

ac-08 principle note (load-bearing): orbit-shaped rules belong in METHOD.md (substrate vocabulary, pipeline shape, BLUF prose skeleton, agent-self-learning patterns, state-persistence rules, the card-vs-choice discrimination) — they are the substrate distillation downstream projects @-import. Project-specific repo discipline belongs in project CLAUDE.md (git push/pull, CI gates, branch naming, repo intro paragraph, expanded STYLE.md if any). The discrimination test for any rule: 'does this rule remain true if the project changes language, framework, or branching model?' If yes → METHOD.md. If no → project CLAUDE.md. Future audits don't re-derive this — they apply it.
